title: Object Types, Interfaces, Aliases, and Structural Compatibility · Inference Brief: Structural Compatibility
summary: Design public service records that evolve without silently accepting misspelled, mutable, or incompatible fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts structural compatibility increment in an evolvable service-record contract library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.
Choose interface or type alias from extension, unions, declaration merging, diagnostics, and API intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Structural Compatibility
steps.instruction:
theory case — A scheduling record has id "job-6", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time.
